criterion: Sprint 17 SDV §4 C4 — Security-cascade integration test (GAP-7), real-TPM tier
closes: Sprint-17 SWAGR MINOR-2 — real-TPM cascade reported green by builder I but previously unverified in-tree; this is the independent on-chip re-verification with captured evidence.

host.tpm: TPM 2.0 via Microsoft Platform Crypto Provider

cascade_steps_verified: 1. provision-key — real TPM signing-key ceremony (non-exportable key + public-half SPKI export)
2. cert-gen — provision_per_boot_certs (real per-boot CA + per-service mTLS certs)
3. boot — DEK envelope build/persist/unseal/reload via real TpmSealer (RSA-2048 OAEP)
4. per-boot mint — second provisioning rotates to distinct cert material (serial/key/sig)
5. mTLS handshake — real CERT_REQUIRED mutual TLS over minted certs; peer CN verified == gateway client CN
6. TPM-signed audit record — AuditLog append + full chain verify via TpmRecordSigner (ECDSA P-256, dedicated audit key)
not_measured: Latency/throughput — this is a security-correctness cascade, not a performance benchmark.
Co-resident cost under concurrent model load — the cascade is security-material only (no GPU/model load).
isolation: tmp_path only; root conftest redirects LOCALAPPDATA/HOME; the real certs/ dir and DEK keystore are not touched.
notes: Real-TPM tier; the SoftwareSealer stand-in tier remains green in the standing gate (gate-deselected here via the hardware marker).
Idempotent: reuses the operator's production audit key (BlarAI-Audit-Signing-Key-v1); provisions + tears down only the two cascade-scoped test keys, leaving no stray persisted keys.
